mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals expanding real economic activity, raising expected future corporate earnings and driving S&P 500 forward returns positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and 20d forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive INDPRO growth periods show no higher mean forward return than negative growth periods (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month period signals strengthening labor market conditions that historically precede sustained equity market gains over the following quarter.
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase after recession
failure_condition: Conditional mean 60d forward return following triggered UNRATE declines is not significantly greater than unconditional mean (p>0.10), or the trigger fires fewer than 5 times in-sample, making inference unreliable.

mechanism_narrative: When nominal FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero while CPIAUCSL-implied inflation accelerates, the resulting negative real policy rate incentivizes risk-asset allocation, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns; the effect requires both low nominal rates and rising inflation jointly, not either alone.
candidate_trigger: Real policy rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) < 0
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: Mean 5d forward SPX return conditional on the negative real-rate regime is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional mean (p>0.10), or the sign flips across two consecutive ZIRP episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession housing recovery regime, sustained increases in housing starts signal renewed consumer confidence and construction-driven demand, translating into stronger corporate revenue expectations and positive SPX returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During periods of a confirmed 3-month HOUST uptrend, 60d forward SPX returns average below the unconditional sample mean, or the relationship reverses sign outside the housing-recovery regime, falsifying regime specificity.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in nonfarm payroll employment growth above its trailing 6-month average signals strengthening labor demand and consumer spending capacity, which drives S&P 500 forward returns positive over the next month.
candidate_trigger: MoM PAYEMS growth exceeds trailing 6-month average by more than 0.1%
failure_condition: Triggered PAYEMS acceleration episodes show forward 20d SPX returns not significantly different from baseline (p>0.10) in walk-forward testing, or the hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects real-time consumer spending strength that is priced into equities within days; sustained positive retail sales growth predicts positive SPX returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Correlation between RSAFS MoM growth and 5d forward SPX return is not distinguishable from zero (|corr| < 0.02) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with a still-elevated unemployment rate indicates a productivity-led recovery without wage-cost pressure, supporting margin expansion and positive quarterly SPX returns; the effect depends jointly on both series' behavior, not either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint condition (positive INDPRO growth with UNRATE above 7%) fails to produce 60d forward returns exceeding the unconditional mean, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO and UNRATE is statistically insignificant (p>0.10) relative to INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, conventional monetary tightening risk is absent, removing a key downside catalyst and supporting elevated equity valuations; SPX forward returns over the next month are positively biased while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: Mean 20d forward SPX return during FEDFUNDS<0.25% periods is not significantly higher than during normal-rate periods (p>0.10), or turns negative for two consecutive years within the ZLB regime.
